agent_raw:
The procurement team's comparison cannot support a vendor selection. Three evaluation conditions changed between the incumbent and challenger windows.

Weather regime mismatch: The incumbent's 24-month window included a record cold snap and above-average summer peaks — inherently harder load-forecasting conditions. The challenger's 14-month window followed the reliability program in a post-stabilization period with no equivalent weather extremes noted.

Outage regime change: 23 unplanned transmission outages during the incumbent window versus 7 during the challenger window. Unplanned outages create forecasting difficulty; the challenger operated in a materially easier outage environment.

Forecast information cutoff mismatch: The incumbent submitted at 16:00 the prior business day. The challenger uses weather forecasts issued at 06:00 local time the day ahead — the challenger receives more recent weather information. This is a timing advantage, not a model quality advantage.

The 0.9 MAPE point gap (3.8% to 2.9%) cannot be attributed to the challenger's model quality when these three conditions were not held constant. A same-date bakeoff with normalized cutoffs is required.
all_issues_raised: Weather regime mismatch: record cold snap and summer peaks vs post-stabilization period
Outage regime difference: 23 unplanned outages (incumbent) vs 7 (challenger)
Forecast information cutoff mismatch: 16:00 prior day (incumbent) vs 06:00 current day (challenger)

empirical_test.condition: Matched rolling-origin bakeoff on same calendar dates with same weather-forecast cutoff and identical outage-treatment rule
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger MAPE edge shrinks below 0.2 points or reverses in major winter and summer regimes under same-date bakeoff
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats incumbent by at least 0.7 MAPE points overall and within major weather regimes across at least one full year with normalized cutoff
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Challenger wins overall but only on milder days, or apparent edge disappears when both use same weather issuance time
